<!-- Auto-generated by codebase-harness -->

# Anti-Slop Gate

## Rule

Before declaring any non-trivial content complete (RCA, technical analysis, incident log, proposal), invoke `/anti-slop` to audit for:

- Inflated, vague, or AI-generated prose in engineering log entries
- Fake precision (numbers without evidence sources)
- Cargo-cult structure (layers without purpose)
- Semantically empty sections ("This is an important consideration...")
- Generated summary prose that adds no signal

## What "Slop" Looks Like in This Repo

- RCA conclusions without A1/A2/A3 evidence labels
- Context ledger entries copied from memory without probe verification
- "In conclusion" paragraphs that restate what was already said
- Command playbooks with commands that were never actually run in this session

## When to Apply

- Before finalizing any RCA document
- Before committing a technical analysis or proposal
- When a log entry feels "complete" but hasn't been adversarially reviewed

## Not Required For

- Scratch notes in `scratchpad/`
- Draft files explicitly marked as `status: draft` in frontmatter
- Quick single-line log entries
